text:
Israel:
the possibility of an eruption in the Middle East is more serious now than it has been for some time, reportedly believe Israel should "watch and wait" and be prepared to occupy West Jordan. The Israelis apparently believe that UAR intervention in Iraq would result in chaos in the area, possibly leading to Nasir's domination of Jordan. In a press interview on 3 November, Prime Minister Ben-Gurion warned the UAR that any attempt to stage a coup in Iraq would compel Israel to "reserve freedom of action." Nasir has been concerned about Israel's attitude and has hinted at a desire for Western restraint of Israel in the event the UAR becomes involved in Iraq. (Page 3)

UAR-Iraq:
The Cairo authorities presumably fear that continued activity would provide the Iraqis with an excuse to expel additional UAR intelligence personnel operating under diplomatic cover

text: Saudi Arabia: The former Saudi minister of commerce and a group of other influential Saudis outside the royal family hope to obtain real control of the Saudi government if Crown Prince Faysal's illness keeps him out of the country beyond the middle of November. Apparently hoping to capitalize on King Saud's desire to regain power from Faysal, they aim to persuade the King to reassert his authority on 16 November when the princes who sit on the council of ministers reassemble. According to their plan, the king will dissolve the council if it refuses to support him and will send key senior princes to ambassadorial posts abroad. The group plans to assume the important cabinet posts and apparently expects to use Saud as a figurehead, keeping him in line by means of control over Saudi finances
